body.title: Web UI: device on/off toggle via circuit breaker (writes state, supervisor reconciles)
body.description:
Enable 'DS.0 normally off — toggle it on in the web UI and nothing else is needed' use case. The web server is a separate process from the rack supervisor; it cannot call shim.start() directly. Instead: toggle writes circuit_state.json; the rack supervisor's ensure_daemon_running() loop reconciles within one poll cycle (30s).

Changes:
1. New GET /api/devices endpoint: reads circuit_state.json + announced worker json files; returns list of {worker_id, circuit (OPEN/CLOSED), announced (bool), available (bool)}
2. New POST /api/devices/<worker_id>/toggle endpoint: reads current circuit state, flips it, writes circuit_state.json atomically (tmp+rename). Returns new state.
3. Web UI device card: shows each shim-managed slot (CC.0, CC.1, DS.0) with on/off badge and toggle button. Calls POST /api/devices/<id>/toggle on click.
4. ensure_daemon_running() bidirectional fix in worker_shim.py: OPEN + thread alive → self.stop(); CLOSED + thread dead → self.start(). (This may ship with T-ccworker-shim-listener-thread but is a completion criterion for THIS ticket.)

The toggle does NOT call any shim method directly. The supervisor reconciles within its next poll cycle. The web UI optimistically shows the new state after POST response.

**Affected files:**
devices/web_server/ (new endpoints + device card component),
devices/claude/worker_shim.py (ensure_daemon_running bidirectional — may already land in T-ccworker-shim-listener-thread),
circuit_state.json path (already at _CIRCUIT_STATE_FILE in worker_shim.py)

**Design rules:** button pattern (atomic state owner — circuit_state.json; callers just write it); log-every-state-change; no-sqlite; no Postgres dependency for circuit state (flat-file only — must work before DB is up)

**Scope boundary:** Device on/off toggle for shim-managed slots only (CC.0, CC.1, DS.0). Out of scope: full rack health dashboard, Granny circuit breaker UI, DickSimnel launch config (separate), real-time websocket push (polling is fine).

**Test plan:** GET /api/devices returns correct circuit state; POST /api/devices/DS.0/toggle flips OPEN→CLOSED in circuit_state.json; test idempotent toggle; test endpoint graceful when circuit_state.json absent (defaults all to CLOSED); browser smoke test: toggle DS.0 off → on, verify web UI badge changes and Granny dispatch resumes within 30s.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
[error] PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write): The skeleton must never depend on Postgres — skeleto — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-004 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write))
…and 37 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: GET /api/devices + POST /api/devices/{id}/toggle + /devices HTML page + atomic _write_circuit_state + nav link, 10 tests
